daily_fact: Comedian Pete Davidson is listing another New York property while selling his Staten Island condo, highlighting continued real estate activity tied to the borough.
topline_md: NYC’s latest headlines center on public safety and quality-of-life concerns—from missing Brooklyn sisters, Bronx shootings and an East Harlem police shooting to housing, transit and infrastructure complaints—while officials also weigh major development, highway expansion and a cloudier economic outlook.

items.title: State bill targets highway expansions near vulnerable New Yorkers
items.why_it_matters: The proposal could reshape major road projects and reduce pollution burdens in affected city neighborhoods.

items.title: AOC presses Hochul to drop Cross Bronx Expressway widening
items.why_it_matters: The fight could affect traffic, air quality, and health for Bronx residents living near the expressway.

items.title: Job losses and Wall Street weakness darken NYC outlook
items.why_it_matters: A weaker economy can hit city jobs, tax revenue, services, and household finances across the five boroughs.

items.title: Staten Island volunteer fire companies still lack city funding
items.why_it_matters: Funding gaps could strain emergency response capacity despite thousands of 911 calls on Staten Island.

items.title: Thousands of New Yorkers affected by tax return typo
items.why_it_matters: Errors in tax processing can delay refunds and create financial headaches for affected residents.

items.title: RXR seeks permits for 95-story tower by Grand Central
items.why_it_matters: A major Midtown project could influence jobs, transit demand, skyline development, and nearby real estate.

items.title: Affordable housing lottery opens for East New York development
items.why_it_matters: New affordable apartments can expand housing options for eligible New Yorkers facing high rents.

items.title: City pushes applications for free NYC child care
items.why_it_matters: More families may access free child care, reducing costs and helping parents stay employed.

items.title: NYC expands Rikers workforce training programs
items.why_it_matters: Job training for people in custody can improve reentry outcomes and reduce future unemployment.

items.title: Bronx tenant reports rat-infested apartment health fears
items.why_it_matters: Severe housing conditions can threaten tenant health and highlight enforcement gaps in unsafe buildings.

items.title: State eases restrictions on eating Hudson River fish
items.why_it_matters: Updated fish consumption guidance affects anglers and families relying on local catches for food.

items.title: Car-free Earth Day opens streets citywide April 25
items.why_it_matters: Street closures and open-streets programming will affect travel and offer free public space across all five boroughs.

items.title: Piping plover nesting season begins on NYC beaches
items.why_it_matters: Beach access and pet rules may change as the city protects endangered birds during nesting season.

items.title: Upper West Side pothole season worsens street conditions
items.why_it_matters: Road damage can disrupt driving, biking and bus trips while raising safety concerns for neighborhood residents.

items.title: Manhattan queer bookstore may be saved by Bushwick shop
items.why_it_matters: A rescue effort could preserve an important LGBTQ cultural space and community gathering spot in Manhattan.

items.title: MoMA PS1 marks 50 years with anniversary celebrations
items.why_it_matters: The milestone brings new cultural programming to Queens and highlights a major local arts institution.
